source.notes:
Topline: Mid-market product analytics vendor evaluating Redwood for customer-facing search/chat and internal reranking. Started on Hosted API (quick PoC) -> moved to short Dedicated POC to validate latency and unit economics. Team is product-led; decision driven by engineering + finance.

PoC summary: 2-week Dedicated POC in Jan 2025 using a 3-node reserved pool (NVIDIA-A100-equivalent) with burst to 5 nodes for peak. Ingested ~1.2M docs for embeddings+search; ran chat flows and reranking pipelines. Verified end-to-end integration via Redwood JS SDK and internal platform connectors.

Performance & requirements (from engineering):
- Target: p95 chat latency < 200ms for 1-2 turn prompts; sustained throughput target 400-600 req/s for batched embeddings/reranking.
- KV cache/prefix caching required for top-N user flows.
- Model preference: open variants (Mistral/llama-family) for cost; will evaluate custom fine-tuned model on Dedicated.

Security & compliance notes:
- Must have SOC2 Type II, SSO/SAML, audit logs to meet enterprise customer needs.
- Data residency: certain EU customer data must be pinned to EU region (legal request). Looking at Redwood Private for full residency but first exploring Dedicated with region pinning.

Pricing/packaging negotiation (current state):
- Initial Redwood draft: 3-node committed pool (monthly) with ability to burst to 5 nodes; ramp schedule proposed: 50% of commit in month 1, 75% month 2, 100% month 3. Overage billed at +30% to committed rate.
- SableQuartz counter: wants flat overage at +15% and ramp to start at 75% month 1. Also asked for 10% discount on list for 12-mo commit and a guaranteed cost per GPU-hour floor.
- Additional asks: ability to add temporary burst capacity for seasonal spikes (marketing demo days) with predictable billing caps, and a 30-day trial period of burst hours without charge to validate traffic patterns.
- Legal: request to include data residency clause and explicit clause on model weights (no access to customer fine-tunes).

Customer sentiment & quotes: "Hosted was super easy to get started — SDK + logs made integration trivial. Dedicated is more about predictable cost; finance is pushing for a fixed-ish commit." - Head of Platform.

Internal Redwood play: Jordan (AE) + Priya (SE) did three pricing calls with SableQuartz finance and infra leads. Priya ran perf tests on Jan 22 and provided a p95 heatmap. Jordan sent counter SOW on Feb 5. Legal loop opened 2025-02-07.

Risks: finance wants firm ARR commit; legal needs residency language; performance validation under 600 req/s peak still in progress (stress test scheduled 2025-02-14).

source.notes:
Status: Closed - lost to hyperscaler managed endpoints (AWS).

Summary bullets:
- Late-stage procurement; heavy emphasis from procurement and infra on reducing vendor footprint.
- POC (embeddings + semantic search) ran on Redwood Dedicated in a single VPC subnet (POC cluster spun for 2 weeks). Latency and relevance metrics met internal SLOs.
- Cost model: Redwood estimated ~30-40% TCO advantage at forecasted scale (with caching + batching) but procurement prioritized single-cloud consolidation and integrated billing.
- Security review: Redwood passed technical answers; security team flagged support burden of a second inference vendor despite VPC option. Quote from SecOps: "we can't add another inference vendor into our stack right now — enterprise AWS contract covers managed endpoints and support." 
- Decision: Oakridge procurement selected AWS-managed endpoints (SageMaker/Bedrock) to keep vendor count down and to leverage existing enterprise discount and monitoring integrations.

Timeline (recent activity):
- 2024-11-12: Intro discovery call (Jordan B.) — high-level requirements, use-case fit (embeddings + underwriting chat).
- 2024-11-29: Technical deep dive with SE (Maya C.) — runtime/latency expectations, VPC connectivity discussed.
- 2024-12-08: POC kickoff — Redwood provided dedicated test pool and sample connectors into S3/RedShift.
- 2025-01-18: Security questionnaire submitted; follow-up Q&A with SecOps.
- 2025-01-22: POC wrap & demo (fireflies ff-2025-01-22-oi-poceval). Results: relevance + latency OK; cost projection disputed by procurement.
- 2025-02-06: Procurement decision meeting — confirmed preference for AWS-managed endpoints.
- 2025-02-10: Final email from procurement. "We will onboard with AWS endpoints to simplify ops and unify support. Open to revisit at renewal."

Why we lost (main drivers):
- Procurement policy requiring cloud-native / managed endpoints where possible to reduce vendor management overhead.
- Existing enterprise AWS contract + internal incentives (billing/observability/managed support) made hyperscaler option administratively simpler.
- No strong internal champion to fight consolidation decision — infra prioritized least operational friction.

Objections raised from Oakridge that were not fully resolved:
- Concern about multi-cloud vendor sprawl (their priority) vs. Redwood argument for cost/latency tradeoffs.
- Questions around single-signoff for incident response and vendor SLAs; they preferred a single hyperscaler support path.

title: Oakridge Intelligence Group
preview:
Status: Closed - lost to hyperscaler managed endpoints (AWS).

Summary bullets:
- Late-stage procurement; heavy emphasis from procurement and infra on reducing vendor footprint.
- POC (embeddings + semantic search) ran on Redwood Dedicated in a single VPC subnet (POC cluster spun for 2 weeks). Latency and relevance metrics met internal SLOs.

- Decision: Oakridge procurement selected AWS-managed endpoints (SageMaker/Bedrock) to keep vendor count down and to leverage existing enterprise discount and monitoring integrations.

source.notes:
Summary: Palisade is a homeland-focused contractor building an enterprise search product for state and federal agencies. Primary ask: high-quality RAG for classified/unclassified stores with deterministic latency and provable data isolation.

Key constraints and asks (shorthand):
- Must run in air-gapped on-prem datacenters behind agency firewall; control plane can be private (customer-managed) or hosted in gov-cloud if required.
- Indexing scale: plan to onboard ~30M documents initially, expected growth to 100M+ within 18 months. Concerned about embedding generation cost and storage for dense vectors.
- Latency SLOs: 95th percentile under 300ms for typical query (embedding + rerank), target p50 < 120ms. Most queries are short chat-like queries and doc retrieval.
- Cost sensitivity: procurement expects unit economics analysis (cost/query) for hosted vs dedicated vs private. Requested cost model templates.
- Model preferences: open-model-first (LLaMA-family and RLHF variants), quantized weights acceptable; want option for certified model variants for classified workloads.
- Routing/fallback: need deterministic fallback to CPU-based sparse search if GPU pool unavailable; explicit policy controls for priority routing.
- Security/compliance: strict KMS/HSM integration, FIPS-validated crypto, audit logs with 7+ year retention, SSO via SAML + SCIM, per-route access controls.

Quotes: "We cannot tolerate silent data exfiltration risk — every cross-host call must be auditable," — CISO.

POC ask: deliver an on-prem pilot (air-gapped) for 5M-doc index, evaluate embed throughput and reranking latency, and provide a cost-per-query baseline. SE to propose batching and cache strategies to hit cost/latency targets.

Artifacts: pricing deck, security FAQ, and initial SOW drafts located in linked_drive_docs. Ongoing thread in gmail-thread-2025-01-21-BX9 contains procurement Qs.

Next tactical: finalize SOW and build on-prem pilot timeline. Procurement expects vendor questionnaire answers before demo to committee.

Key account for Q1 push — R&D + Investment Research teams consolidated 3 legacy indices. Preference for dedicated capacity with private control plane. Primary ask: scale to ~30M docs (mixed PDFs, time-series tables) with sub-400ms 95th-percentile rerank latency. Cost sensitivity: goal ~<$0.002/query for vector + rerank on average workload.

Meeting snippets:
- "We need deterministic latency across European trading hours — spikes during market open are non-negotiable."
- Engineer: "want to avoid per-request cold starts; prefix/KV cache critical".

They are evaluating Redwood for ability to: (1) host embeddings at scale with incremental indexing; (2) run a dense-retrieval + cross-encoder rerank pipeline; (3) integrate KMS/HSM and meet EU residency + retention policy. CSM call 2025-01-20 confirmed can move to procurement if SOW matches latency SLA and KMS certs.

2024-12-10 - POC numbers: embeddings vector size 768, avg retrieval latency 60ms, cross-encoder rerank 280ms (p95 420ms) — discussed optimization levers

source.indexing_estimate: ~30M documents, ~1.2B tokens total, expected embedding store ~1.1TB (dense vectors 768d, float16 compressed)
source.throughput_target: sustained 120 QPS during peak windows, burst to 250 QPS
source.cost_per_query_target: target average cost < $0.002 per query (vector search + rerank amortized)
preferred_models: option to fallback to smaller emb models for cold traffic
source.reranking_strategy: Two-stage pipeline: ANN dense retrieval (HNSW, sharded) -> top-50 candidate cross-encoder rerank. Explore distilled cross-encoder to lower latency/cost, with automatic fallback to heuristics under load.
source.storage_and_indexing_tech: Prefer vector indexes that support sharding + incremental updates (FAISS-IVF/HNSW hybrid). Long-term archive to S3 (EU) with warm-hot caching layer for most-accessed docs.
source.recent_poc_findings: Cross-encoder rerank is the dominant cost; quantized 16->8-bit experiments reduced latency 30% but raised small quality delta. Batching and continuous KV caching reduces per-query compute by ~18% in sim tests.
source.procurement_notes: Requested 3-year reserved Dedicated pool options, month-to-month burst, and ability to pin model versions. Legal needs KMS HSM attestation and data-residency clause for EU/UK. Finance asked for line-item TCO projection.
customer_quotes: "We're trying to replace 4 point-solutions — performance and residency are table stakes. If you can prove predictable p95 under load, we can sign." - Head of Research
"Need a clear upgrade path from dev POC to Dedicated with minimal code changes." - Lead Engineer
source.risks: High sensitivity to SLA breaches during market open; integration complexity for HSM/KMS; cost overruns if rerank scale not optimized.
source.win_conditions: Dedicated capacity contract signed + KMS integration verification + SLO: p95 rerank < 400ms in EU for sustained 120 QPS

source.external_next_actions: Share Dedicated SOW draft with procurement; timeline for HSM onboarding (2-4 weeks) and SOC2 artifacts; schedule executive sponsorship call.

source.notes:
Summary: mid-market SaaS (B2B engagement platform). Started on Hosted API to validate model quality and latency. POC results were positive; now moving to Dedicated to get predictable throughput and cost predictability. Key negotiation points revolve around commit levels, burst capacity, overage pricing, and ramp schedule.

Key technical requirements / quotes: 
- Workload: primarily chat-based support agent (context windows ~2k tokens) + periodic reranking for search (embedding+rerank).
- Latency SLO: p95 < 250ms for chat generation in production; peak qps target 800-1,200 after ramp.
- Throughput pattern: weekday peaks, predictable nightly batch jobs for embeddings.
- Model preferences: open model family with moderate fine-tune; willing to use Redwood-verified smaller quantized variants for cost.
- Routing/fallback: prefer same-region fallback to lower-cost model variant; retention controls required for PII removal.

Pricing / commercial notes: 
- They want a 12-month reserved capacity deal but asked for 3-month ramp schedule (40% month1, 70% month2, 100% month3 commit util).
- Strong ask for burst pool: 20-25% burst above committed baseline for seasonal spikes, with a predictable monthly cap.
- Overage model: prefer flat overage rate (per-token) with invoiced monthly; CFO asked if we can do daily throttling instead of heavy overages.
- Discount ask: mid-market benchmark requested 18-25% off list for 12mo commit + upfront payment.
- Want explicit cost modeling for quantized model variants (fp16 vs 4-bit) to evaluate tradeoffs.

Security / compliance notes: 
- Must have SSO (SAML), audit logs retention 2 years, KMS for key management.
- Data residency: primary US region, but legal wants clause preventing cross-border storage for PII.

Sales tactics discussed: 
- Offer staged SOW: month-to-month pilot fee -> 12mo contract with defined ramp schedule -> optional annual renew with deeper discount.
- Include a clear burst allowance and a capped overage rate; provide a billing simulator spreadsheet for CFO.
- Proposed play: 3-tier commit options (base: 100k tokens/day, mid: 250k/day, high: 500k/day) with 20% burst and overage at fixed $/1k tokens.

Quotes from stakeholders: 
- "We liked the POC latency and consistency — next is making the economics predictable for our finance team." — VP Product
- "If we can't get predictable ramp+burst, legal/finance will push back on committing." — CFO

Next steps (owner action items): 
- Maya: circulate updated SOW template and billing simulator by 2025-01-20.
- Diego: finalize performance annex with test harness and accepted p95 targets (due 2025-01-22).
- Jordan: prepare onboarding + CSM plan draft for committed customers to be included in SOW.

Open questions / risks: 
- Confirm whether burst pool can be shared across regions without additional residency exposure.
- Need legal-approved data residency clause wording; security team to propose exact retention controls.
- CFO wants a month-at-a-glance overage cap option (hard cap) — do we support this commercially?

notes: Account summary: BroadHaven is a mid-to-large global quant analytics firm building a live chat + ranking layer for downstream trading tools. Heavy emphasis on millisecond-quality inference and strong audit trail for model inputs/outputs.
Primary ask: Enterprise Dedicated pool with reserved GPUs and contractual SLOs for throughput and latency. They want predictable monthly capacity and hard uptime guarantees tied to financial credits.

Throughput/latency targets: p95 latency target for conversational rfs ~ <150ms (token-level), sustained throughput ~25-35 requests/sec for their peak model (context 8k tokens). Batchable workloads (embeddings) daytime throughput target 1500 reqs/min with async guarantees.

Reserved GPU planning: initial commitment: 12x A100-80GB (preferably single-tenant nodes) with autoscale burst to 18 for up to 6 hours/day. Want placement in us-east-1 and optional eu-west-1 DR. Interested in topology: pinned model replicas + warm KV cache nodes.
SLA negotiation items (customer position):
- Requested uptime: 99.95% monthly (max 21.9 min downtime/month). If breach: cash credit schedule preferred: 5% monthly fee for 99.9-99.95, 15% for 99.5-99.9, 50% for <99.5
- Maintenance windows: cannot have disruptive maintenance Mon-Fri 08:00-20:00 EST. Any maintenance needs 72h notice and a test/fallback window. No forced restarts during trading hours.

- Support SLAs: P1 response = 15 minutes phone + 1 hour actionable update, P2 = 1 hour email + 4 hour update, escalation path to engineering lead within 2 hours. On-call overlap with client PST hours required for first 90 days.
- Service credits vs cash: finance wants option for cash credits (up to agreed cap) instead of platform credit for multi-year contract. Also requests a service-level audit right once per year.

Redwood baseline (current offer discussed): 99.9% uptime, credit schedule: 10% credit for each 0.1% below 99.9 down to a cap of 50%, 48h maintenance notice, P1 30 min response, engineering on-call for severe incidents. Dedicated pricing attached.

Negotiation status: Redwood legal sent v2 SLA Dec 10; BroadHaven redline received Dec 13 with demands on uptime and cash credits. AE + SE reviewed redline 12/14; need to escalate to Revenue Operations and Legal for cash-credit clause and blackout windows.
Recent activity timeline:
- 2024-11-08: Intro call (Rohan) – overview, GA use case, initial sizing (10-14 A100 equiv), interest in Dedicated + Private CP
- 2024-11-21: Architecture deep-dive with SE (Lea) - network/VPC constraints, KMS/HSM discussion; recorded ff_20241121_arch
- 2024-12-03: Demo + throughput POC kickoff (recording ff_20241203_BH_Rohan-demo-1), POC targets agreed: sustain 30 req/s p95 <150ms for 90 min window
- 2024-12-09: Security questionnaire submitted (see SEC-2231). SOC2 + ISO questions largely satisfied, but HSM/KMS PKI lifecycle open item
- 2024-12-13: BroadHaven returns SLA redline; asks for 99.95% and cash credits; requests blackout maintenance hours
- 2024-12-14: Internal pricing/legal alignment meeting; Redwood proposes escalation matrix and optional premium SLA add-on for 99.95% (cost uplift ~ +35%)
- 2024-12-15: AE to schedule legal-to-legal call; draft redline to be sent by Redwood with premium SLA pricing and proposed cash-credit cap

POC notes: POC running on dedicated test pool — initial kernel selection favorable; KV cache warmup needed before hitting p95 targets. SE recommends warm standby nodes and a small prefix cache to meet latency targets.

source.summary: Enterprise evaluation for reserved GPU pool and SLA negotiation focused on uptime, credits, maintenance windows, and support SLAs.
source.notes:
PROSPECT CONTEXT:
- Late-stage AI infrastructure team for a global financial analytics SaaS. Heavy inference on customer data; high sensitivity to downtime.
- Want reserved capacity for predictable pricing and consistent latency. Will consider Private if VPC KMS path delayed.

TECH/REQUIREMENTS (shorthand):
- Throughput: steady 3k-5k tok/s; bursts to 10k for 5m windows.
- Latency SLOs: p95 <=100ms for chat; p99 <=300ms for reranking.
- Reserves: equivalent of 8 A100s reserved; ability to burst +2 nodes for promos.
- Security: SOC2 required, SSO/SAML, KMS key ownership, audit logs + 7y retention discussion.

SLA & CONTRACT NOTES (from Legal + Finance call):
- Palladium wants 99.95% uptime as baseline. Redwood initial draft was 99.9%.
- Credit schedule is a sticking point: Palladium requests credits starting at 99.92% and steeper multipliers for deeper outages. Finance requires explicit minute-based calculations.
- Maintenance window: Palladium will accept one monthly window up to 2 hours local time; refuses ad-hoc restarts outside the window.
- Support: 1-hour response for P1 incidents, 4-hour action; require 24/7 escalation bridge for incidents impacting the SLA.

QUOTES / VOICE OF CUSTOMER:
- "We cannot accept silent platform restarts during business hours — our customers notice and we lose revenue." (VP Eng)
- "Need a clear credit table we can put into our finance model." (Head of Finance)

CURRENT NEGOTIATION STATUS:
- Redwood provided SLA draft v2 on 2024-12-03 (drive link above).
- Palladium returned redlines 2024-12-12: pushed uptime to 99.95%, narrower scheduled maintenance carve-outs, requested faster credits.
- Technical POC results favorable: Redwood POC met targets for p95 latency in controlled runs; throughput still needs validation under burst loads.

RISK & OPPORTUNITIES:
- Risk: Legal may escalate to procurement pause if Redwood won't agree to 99.95% or accept Palladium's credit schedule.
- Opportunity: Dedicated + private combo is sticky — potential multi-year reserved contract if SLA acceptable.

SHORT NEXT STEPS (AE/SE/Legal):
- Redwood to deliver a redlined SLA that proposes compromise uptime (99.92% + path to 99.95% after Q1 optimizations) and a minute-accurate credit table by 2024-12-22.
- SE to run extended burst POC (48h) to validate 10k tok/s bursts and upload results to drive.
- Legal to align on KMS language for customer-managed keys.

2024-12-07: Follow-up — maintenance window preferences captured (monthly 01:00-03:00 local).
2024-12-12: SLA redline review — Palladium countered with 99.95% uptime and 4x credit multiplier.

source.notes:
Overview: enterprise quant trading shop building low-latency signal layer. Team: CTO (Victor Hale), Head of Infra (Rina Patel). We kicked off Dedicated evaluation 2024-09.

Key ask: deterministic tail latency <6ms p50/p95 for short completion paths; batchable work for embeddings & rerank off-path. Heavy throughput during market open windows (13:30-14:30 ET) - want reserved capacity to avoid noisy-neighbor impact.

SLA negotiation highlights: they insist on financial credits for downtime (tiered), 99.95% as baseline target proposed by them, pushing for 99.99% for core inference path. Must have <1 minute failover to fallback model variant. Require 72-hour maintenance notice, 2x per month maintenance window only outside 13:00-15:00 ET.

Security/compliance: strict KMS for keys, audit log retention 2 years, SSO via SAML + SCIM sync. Data residency: logs to remain in us-east region only. Legal wants right to audit infra configurations quarterly.

Procurement notes (shorthand): "legal: credit schedule, termination on 2 consecutive SLA breaches, cap on credits = monthly fee"; procurement wants sample contract redlines from Redwood.

Quote from CTO on 2024-11-21 call: "We need predictability — if it slips during opens we lose millions. Credits are good, but we need guaranteed reserve and fast remediation."

Attachments: pricing deck v3, rough perf-run CSV, SOC2 gap analysis spreadsheet.
sla_requirements: Uptime target: customer requesting 99.95% baseline, debating 99.99% for core paths
Credit schedule: tiered credits based on minutes of downtime; full month credit cap = 100% of monthly recurring fee; request for pro-rata for partial day outages
Maintenance windows: max 2x/month, prefer off-peak ET (outside 13:00-15:00 ET); 72h advance notice
Emergency maintenance: allowed but require immediate rollback option + post-mortem within 48h
Support response: P1 <= 15 min initial response, P1 resolution SLA negotiable; P2 <= 2 hours initial response
source.throughput_targets: Peak: 45k tokens/sec sustained for reranking fleet during open; baseline continuous: 8-12k tokens/sec. Expect burst capacity for 30-45 minutes during market open. Reserved planning: initial reserved GPU fleet = 8 x A100-equivalent, autoscale headroom +2 units for bursts; want clear scaling lead times.
source.latency_slos: Core inference path target: p50 <4ms, p95 <6ms for short completion (<64 tokens). Non-critical batch paths (embeddings/rerank) p95 <250ms acceptable. End-to-end cold-start SLO for model swap <1s.
source.reserved_gpu_plan: Initial commit: 8 GPUs reserved (A100/80GB class). Growth plan: +4 GPUs after Q2 post-onboarding (conditional on perf validation). Billing: monthly reserved fee + usage overage buckets. Customer requests transparent GPU-to-TPS mapping and recommended kernel/quantization profile.
source.negotiation_summary: Week-by-week cadence established. Legal and infra deep-dive mid-Nov. Open items: final uptime %, exact credit math (minutes -> %), maintenance notification window, P1 response/resolution timeline, right-to-audit language. Redwood to provide sample SLA + runbook by 2024-12-10. AE to push procurement checklist to accelerate sign-off.

notes: 9/18: Intro call — product/infra overview (Ethan + Diego). HelmForge building autonomy stack for industrial vehicles; heavy on latency-sensitive decision loops.
9/25: Shared private deployment brief + compliance checklist. Customer asked for audit log retention and HSM/KMS integration.
10/03: Technical deep-dive with infra team. Key ask: ability to run in their GPU cluster behind strict egress rules; preferred runtime: vLLM or Triton-backed TensorRT-LLM.
10/12: Demo — hosted API performance looked good; Diego ran throughput sims vs their sample traces (drive cycle logs). Fireflies id ff_2024-10-12_helmforge_demo.
10/20: Security review requested on SSO/SAML, ISO attestations. Shared drive doc: helmforge_security_deck_v2.pdf. Customer flagged SOC/ISO mismatch for hosted control plane.
11/05: POC kickoff — 2-week litmus POC to measure latency/kv cache hit rates with 8k context. Security call recorded ff_2024-11-05_security_call.

11/19: POC results — Redwood ded. nodes met latency targets but infra cost estimate higher than internal projection. Customer commented: 'We can shave costs running inference on our DGX fleet with Triton.'
11/25: Procurement engaged. CFO pushed for TCO comparison vs self-host. Internal infra team proposed vLLM + TGI for orchestration + TensorRT-LLM for optimized kernels.
11/29: Final evaluation meeting. No exec champion surfaced to approve the premium for managed private control plane. Concerns: long-term unit economics, full data control, air-gap requirements.
12/01: Customer decision — chosen path: self-hosting stack (vLLM/TGI/TensorRT-LLM) to maximize control and reduce per-token costs. Loss reason logged: self-hosted_choice + budget priority.

Action items: Send detailed loss digest (what we delivered in POC, cost delta breakdown), propose phased partner model (monitoring/Optimize add-on) — customer asked to keep us in the loop for observability connectors.
Quote captured from head of infra: 'Great platform, but we already have the GPU estate and the team to run it; we need full control and lower unit cost.'
Open: follow-up in 90 days to offer Optimize retrofit and hybrid routing (if they run out of capacity). Also offer security integration workshop for future private deployments.

2024-09-12 - Deep-dive (SE): scope for POC defined; ingestion pipeline, connectors, and allowed egress discussed (none outside SCIF).

use_case_summary: Primary: classified-doc-search and retrieval across multiple sealed repositories (embeddings + reranking).
Secondary: incident-response chat assistant for duty officers that can synthesize procedures and generate playbooks from policy documents (low-latency chat).
Tertiary: automated daily briefings and compliance reports that compile events and annotate with regulatory citations (batch jobs, lower latency tolerance).
technical_requirements: Must run entirely on-prem inside customer-controlled racks; no outbound network egress from production inference cluster.
Air-gap install option: support for physical media model ingestion, signed docker/artifact bundles, and validated image provenance.
KMS/HSM: integrate with on-prem HSM (PKCS#11) for model keys and secrets; support FIPS 140 validated crypto.
Data handling: strict retention controls, audit logging per request, ability to redact PII and provide retention-flagging attributes in logs.
Hardware: target 4x A100-equivalent GPUs for baseline POC; later scale to reserved capacity pool for predictable throughput.
Latency/throughput targets: median chat RTT < 350ms for <512-token requests inside lab; embeddings throughput 250 qps during peak batch windows.
Routing/fallback: prefer highest-accuracy model variant on-prem; fallback to smaller quantized model only within the air-gapped environment (no outbound model fallback).
poc_plan_and_milestones: POC length: 6 weeks in isolated lab (synthetic + redacted historical docs).
Week 0: network access review, install plan, signed acceptance criteria.
Week 1-2: on-site install of Private control plane + inference nodes in customer lab, HSM integration test.
Week 3-4: ingest test corpus, build embeddings index, run reranking experiments, measure latency under representative load.
Week 5: security validation (audit logs, retention, FIPS/HSM checks) with ISSO present.
Week 6: joint runbook demo, false-positive/false-negative review, handoff to procurement for SOW.
Acceptance criteria: 1) no outbound network calls during inference, 2) per-request audit logs captured and exportable, 3) median chat RTT < 350ms at target load.
commercial_notes: Customer requested a modular pricing line for 'air-gap support' (one-time) + 'on-site commissioning' + annual support SLA.
Estimated ARR range provided on 2024-11-20 deck; higher tier includes extended observability retention and on-call engineering days.
Procurement timeline: expect final PO after POC sign-off and internal budget cycle in Jan-Feb 2025.
security_and_compliance_notes: ISSO requires vendor to provide reproducible SBOMs and signed artifact manifests for all runtime images.
Customer will perform hardware-level attestation; need to support remote attestation flow or provide manual attestation artifacts for on-site install.
Audit requirements: immutable per-request logs with tamper-evidence; 2-year retention in offline archival store.
Data residency: all data, indices, and logs must remain on-prem in designated SCIF; backups must be air-gapped media only.
sales_notes_and_quotes: AE/Ethan: 'They are motivated to move but extremely procedural — security gating is going to drive timeline.'
SE/Priya: 'Workload is heavy on embeddings+reranking; recommend quantization only after performance regression tests.'
Finance: initial quote includes 3x on-site eng days + one-time air-gap packaging fee; customer asked for a breakdown by line item.
